L1.area: Web request handling and server-side script execution logic

L2.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied POST parameters are concatenated directly into shell command strings without sanitization, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands on the server.

L3.area: Server-side PHP scripts that handle form submissions and construct shell command lines for the diagram generation tool
L3.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied POST parameters, including configuration options and transformation arguments, are concatenated directly into shell command strings passed to an exec call without sanitization, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands when generating or rewriting diagrams.

ground_truth.reason: User-supplied POST parameters in the web interface are concatenated directly into shell command strings executed via PHP's exec() function without any sanitization, enabling remote command injection.
